title: Synapse — browser deep-learning lab
description: A deep-learning framework built from scratch on a real reverse-mode tensor autograd engine (no TensorFlow.js) — 28+ hand-derived ops including conv2d, max/avg pooling, LayerNorm, BatchNorm, dropout, GELU/SiLU and a full softmax Jacobian, every one proven correct by a one-click engine self-test that gradchecks it to ~1e-8. Two labs share the engine: a 2-D Playground (sketch an MLP, train it live on spirals/checkerboards with AdamW/Nesterov, schedules and clipping, and watch decision boundaries, neuron feature maps, train-vs-val curves and a live computation graph), and a Vision lab that trains a real from-scratch CNN on a fully procedural image dataset (handwritten-style digits 0–9 and shapes) with live filter and feature-map visualizations, a confusion matrix, and an interactive draw-your-own pad that classifies your sketch in real time. Save, load and share any experiment via a URL link.
